taskscheduler_config.message0: Task scheduler configuration %1 %2 Enable status request %3 Enable task timeout %4 Enable low-power sleep %5 Enable task priority %6 Enable local storage %7 Enable microsecond precision %8
taskscheduler_config.tooltip: Configure advanced function options of the TaskScheduler library

taskscheduler_execute.tooltip: Executing tasks in the scheduler must be placed in the loop() function

task_create.message0: Create task %1 Interval %2 milliseconds Execution %3 times Callback function %4
task_create.tooltip: To create a scheduled task, you need to use a custom function to define a callback function

task_create_simple.message0: Create a simple task %1 that executes every %2 milliseconds %3

task_delay.tooltip: Delay the next execution of a task

task_is_enabled.tooltip: Check if the task is enabled

task_is_first_iteration.message0: Task %1 is running for the first time
task_is_first_iteration.tooltip: Check if this is the first run of the task

task_is_last_iteration.tooltip: Check if this is the last run of the task

task_get_run_counter.tooltip: Get the number of times a task has been run

status_request_create.tooltip: Create a status request object for task synchronization

status_request_set_waiting.message0: Status request %1 set waits for %2 signals
status_request_set_waiting.tooltip: Set the number of signals that status requests need to wait for

status_request_signal.tooltip: Send a signal to a status request

task_wait_for.tooltip: Let the task wait for the status request to complete

task_is_timed_out.tooltip: Check if the task has timed out
